title: CleanSpark Stock Climbing Ahead of Q2 Earnings Announcement
text: CleanSpark shares were trading up 3.59% at $14.71 on Monday, with momentum building ahead of the company's fiscal Q2 earnings release. Analysts anticipate CleanSpark will report a quarterly loss of 23 cents per share on revenue of $139.24 million. The timing is significant as the company transitions from a single-focus Bitcoin miner into a diversified infrastructure operator. In Q1 (ended Dec. 31, 2025), CleanSpark generated $181.2 million in revenue, up 11.6% year-over-year. However, the company posted a net loss of $378.7 million ($1.35 per share), compared to prior-year net income of $246.8 million (85 cents per share). Adjusted EBITDA swung to negative $295.4 million from positive $321.6 million, reflecting heavy infrastructure expansion and AI data center buildout investments. Management characterizes the losses as part of a planned transformation. CEO Matt Schultz noted the company maintains one of the strongest balance sheets in the sector with increasingly scarce land-and-power assets. President and CFO Gary Vecchiarelli emphasized the company is no longer a one-dimensional miner, leveraging three revenue engines: Bitcoin mining cash flow, AI infrastructure monetization, and digital asset optimization. As of Dec. 31, 2025, CleanSpark held $458.1 million in cash, $1.0 billion in Bitcoin holdings, and $1.3 billion in working capital against total assets of $3.3 billion.
